texto: I’d watched the election of Barack Obama with them, in Harlem: the celebration had spilled out onto the streets and erupted into dancing, outdoor champagne-drinking, euphoria. This [the 1/21/17 Women's March on Washington, DC] was different. It was like laughter at a funeral—what else can you do but hold on to who you are and who you love? What can you do but try to stay sane and fight like hell for what life is all about?
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Sarah Larson: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “I’d watched the election of Barack Obama with them, in Harlem: the celebration had spilled out on…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que humor, democracy, donald-trump pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de donald-trump e elections.
